manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: park, bugle, stadium, fall, dance, centaur, calf, screen, plastic.
Your opponent's team words are: mouse, compound, concert, octopus, opera, pin, rock, dress.
Distractor words are: gas, bottle, air.
The kill words are: undertaker, net, press, europe, well.

message.content:
CLUE: Performance
TARGETS: stadium, dance, bugle
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words, not opponent, distractor, or kill. Must avoid clue being any of the listed words or containing parts of them. Provide CLUE: WORD and TARGETS: list. Must be short, only that format.

Our team words: park, bugle, stadium, fall, dance, centaur, calf, screen, plastic.

We can choose any subset. Want likely multiple. Need clue that is semantically linked.

Potential clue: "Greek" relates to centaur, maybe also calf (as in young goat? not Greek). "Myth" relates centaur. "Sports" relates stadium, park, maybe bugle (military), fall? Not.

"Outdoor" relates park, stadium, fall (season), maybe calf? Not.

"Sound" relates bugle, screen? Actually screen shows movies sound, but not.

"Film" relates screen, plastic (film plastic), maybe stadium (films shot), not.

"Concert" is opponent word cannot be clue.

"Music" relates bugle, dance, maybe stadium (concerts), but caution not opponent.

We can choose few safe.

Maybe clue: "Performance" relates stadium, dance, bugle (military performance), screen (movie performance). Could be okay.

Check if any opponent words also linked to "performance" - concert, opera, etc. That might be risky but as long as not guessed incorrectly.

But we just give clue and targets.

Let's choose three: stadium, dance, bugle. Clue "Performance". It's not in any list.

Check: bugle is a performance instrument, dance is performance, stadium hosts performances. Acceptable.

Return format.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 3 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'performance'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

screen, press, well, europe, net, bugle, bottle, octopus, rock, fall, concert, plastic, dance, park, dress, undertaker, compound, calf, opera, air, gas, pin, stadium, centaur, mouse

message.reasoning:
We need to output "GUESS: " followed by up to 3 words from the list that best relate to 'performance'. List: screen, press, well, europe, net, bugle, bottle, octopus, rock, fall, concert, plastic, dance, park, dress, undertaker, compound, calf, opera, air, gas, pin, stadium, centaur, mouse.

Words related to performance: concert, dance, opera, stadium (performance venue), press (performance review?), screen (performance in movies). Up to 3 words, best relate: concert, dance, opera. Provide comma-separated.

Thus answer: GUESS: concert, dance, opera
